catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q1.explanation:
Câu:
“tools made from ___”

Đoạn:
“primitive tools, which may have been fashioned out of deer antlers”

→ tools để đào ditch/henge có thể làm bằng sừng hươu.

→ Đáp án: deer antlers.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q2.explanation:
Câu:
“___ may have been arranged in deep pits”

Đoạn:
“Deep pits ... may have once held a ring of timber posts”

→ timber posts được đặt trong các hố bên trong circle.

→ Đáp án: timber posts

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q3.explanation:
Câu:
“builders used ___ to make sledges and rollers”

Đoạn:
“fashioned sledges and rollers out of tree trunks”

→ tree trunks được dùng làm rollers để vận chuyển stones.

→ Đáp án: tree trunks.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q4.explanation:
Câu:
“___ pulled them on giant baskets”

Đoạn:
“hauled by oxen”

→ oxen kéo wicker baskets chứa stones.

→ Đáp án: oxen.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q5.explanation:
Câu:
“they were brought from Wales by ___”

Đoạn:
“it was glaciers, not humans, that carried the bluestones”

→ geological theory cho rằng glaciers di chuyển stones.

→ Đáp án: glaciers

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q6.explanation:
Câu:
“builders were Celtic ___”

Đoạn:
“Stonehenge was the work of druids”

→ Aubrey tin rằng druids xây Stonehenge.

→ Đáp án: druids.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q7.explanation:
Câu:
“used as a ___ site”

Đoạn:
“served the function of burial ground”

→ burial ground = burial site.

→ Đáp án: burial.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q8.explanation:
Câu:
“worked as a kind of ___”

Đoạn:
“operated as a form of calendar”

→ Hawkins cho rằng Stonehenge dùng như calendar thiên văn.

→ Đáp án: calendar.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q9.explanation:
Statement:
“sandstone slabs were placed in both outer and middle areas”

Đoạn:

“outer crescent or ring”
“trilithons ... in the centre”

→ sandstone slabs có ở outer ring và centre.

→ TRUE

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q10.explanation:
Statement:
“bluestones stood in the same spot until 1600 BCE”

Đoạn:
“bluestones ... being repositioned multiple times”

→ repositioned = moved many times.

→ không đứng yên một chỗ.

→ FALSE.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q11.explanation:
Statement:
“Aubrey’s claim was supported by 20th-century findings”

Đoạn:
“radiocarbon dating demonstrated that Stonehenge stood more than 1,000 years before the Celts”

→ findings bác bỏ theory của Aubrey, vì druids/Celts xuất hiện muộn hơn Stonehenge.

→ FALSE.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q12.explanation:
Statement:
“constructed by different groups of people”

Đoạn:
“several distinct tribes of people contributed”

và:
“Bones, tools and other artefacts ... support this hypothesis.”

→ archaeological evidence ủng hộ idea rằng nhiều nhóm người tham gia xây dựng.

→ TRUE.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-1-q13.explanation:
Statement:
“Criticism ... came mainly from other astronomers”

Đoạn chỉ nói:
“critics maintain that...”

→ không nói critics là ai, cũng không nói “mainly astronomers”.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q14.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“What point does the writer make about AI?”

Đoạn 1:

hiện tại AI mới là “narrow AI”
nhưng “many experts believe this restriction is very temporary”

→ writer cho rằng giới hạn hiện tại của AI sẽ không kéo dài lâu.

→ Đáp án C: future AI unlikely to have limitations.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q15.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“What is the writer doing in paragraph 2?”

Đoạn 2 giải thích:

human brains có nhiều physical constraints
machines không bị giới hạn như humans

→ machines eventually outperform humans.

→ Đáp án A.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q16.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“Why mention King Midas?”

Đoạn:
“Midas ... wished that everything he touched turned to gold”

→ wish được diễn đạt không cẩn thận nên gây disaster.

→ analogy cho AI:
nếu goals badly defined → dangerous consequences.

→ Đáp án B

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q17.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“What challenge in paragraph 4?”

Đoạn:
“One thing that makes this task difficult is that we are far from reliably human-friendly ourselves.”

→ problem là humans themselves are flawed.

→ difficult to ensure AI more ethical/human-friendly than us.

→ Đáp án D

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q18.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“What does writer suggest in paragraph 5?”

Đoạn:
“they’ll be better than us at finding them”

→ machines may surpass humans morally/intellectually.

→ Đáp án C: machines superior in some respects.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q19.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“best summary of paragraph 6?”

Paragraph 6:

humans are tribal/conflicted
often ignore suffering
difficult to teach machines ideal morality

→ human weaknesses make programming AI harder.

→ Đáp án D.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q20.explanation:
Statement:
“Machines ... may prevent us promoting community interests.”

Đoạn:
“We might lose our freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities”

→ exact same meaning.

→ YES.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q21.explanation:
Statement:
“Silicon police would need to exist in large numbers...”

Bài chỉ nói:
“They might be so good at doing it...”

→ không nói cần large numbers.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q22.explanation:
Statement:
“Many people are comfortable ... restricted by machines.”

Đoạn:
“few of us are likely to welcome such a future.”

→ most people would NOT welcome it.

→ NO.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q23.explanation:
Statement:
“we all need to work together”

Đoạn:
“it will require a cooperative spirit”

→ cooperation needed to ensure safe AI.

→ YES.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q24.explanation:
available resources

Đoạn:
“how resources are used in our National Health Service”

→ resources allocated in health system.

→ Đáp án: resources

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q25.explanation:
medical practitioners

Đoạn:
“depriving some humans (e.g. senior doctors)”

→ senior doctors = medical practitioners.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-2-q26.explanation:
professional authority

Đoạn:
“the control they presently enjoy”

→ control over decisions = professional authority.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q27.explanation:
Bước 1: Tìm từ khóa chính: first referred, genius, 500 years ago.
Bước 2: Định vị thông tin trong bài đọc: Đoạn 1 có viết: "The word ‘genius’ is universally associated with the name of Leonardo da Vinci... Five hundred years have passed since Leonardo died..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc nói rằng từ "thiên tài" được gắn liền với tên tuổi của ông trên toàn thế giới và ông đã mất cách đây 500 năm. Tuy nhiên, bài viết không hề đề cập đến việc người ta lần đầu tiên (first) gọi ông là thiên tài từ khi nào (có phải chính xác vào 500 năm trước hay không).
Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q28.explanation:
Statement:
“current climate crisis ... cause more deaths than the plague”

Bài nói climate crisis sẽ gây:

displacement
extinctions
death

Nhưng không so sánh số người chết với plague.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q29.explanation:
Statement:
“Some challenges today can be compared to earlier times.”

Đoạn:
“Then, as now, radical solutions were called for”

→ writer directly compares Renaissance crisis với modern climate crisis.

→ TRUE.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q30.explanation:
Statement:
“ideal city was constructed”

Đoạn:
“would remain unfulfilled”

→ project never built because of excessive costs.

→ FALSE.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q31.explanation:
Statement:
“Poor town planning is major contributor to climate change.”

Đoạn:
“unsustainable urban models are a key cause of global climate change today”

→ exact same meaning.

→ TRUE.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q32.explanation:
Statement:
“local people fought against changes”

Bài chỉ nói:

Pienza and Ferrara were redesigned/expanded

Không có thông tin local resistance.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q33.explanation:
Statement:
“Leonardo kept neat organised records”

Đoạn:
“his disordered way of working with notes and sketches”

→ opposite meaning.

→ FALSE.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q34.explanation:
Câu:
“provide better ___ for trade”

Đoạn:
“He designed the city for the easy transport of goods”

→ transport linked to trade.

→ Đáp án: transport.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q35.explanation:
Câu:
“features ... such as ___ on the exterior”

Đoạn:
“linked with vertical outdoor staircases”

→ outdoor staircases = exterior stairs.

→ Đáp án: staircases.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q36.explanation:
Câu:
“expertise in ___ was evident”

Đoạn:
“fusion of architecture and engineering”

→ canal systems show engineering expertise.

→ Đáp án: engineering.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q37.explanation:
Câu:
“many cities ... follow this ___”

Đoạn:
“a rule still followed in many contemporary cities”

→ Đáp án: rule.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q38.explanation:
Câu:
“some cities from ___ times”

Đoạn:
“some of these features existed in Roman cities”

→ Roman times.

→ Đáp án: Roman.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q39.explanation:
Câu:
“___ is one example of a city redesigned”

Đoạn:
“renovation of Paris”

→ example directly mentioned.

→ Đáp án: Paris.

catalog-cam-18-2-reading-part-3-q40.explanation:
Câu:
“building ___ no longer seems best”

Đoạn cuối:
“compact city, built upwards instead of outwards”

→ writer suggests cities should not expand outwards.

→ Đáp án: outwards.
